title: Writing - Part 1: Space Exploration
description: Your class has been discussing whether governments should continue to fund space exploration programmes.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the arguments in your notes. You should explain which argument you find more persuasive, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Space research has led to vital developments in medicine, materials and communications technology.
The billions spent on space programmes could be better used to solve problems like poverty and disease on Earth.
Space missions capture the public imagination and encourage young people to pursue science and engineering.

structure.introduction: Introduce the ongoing debate about the value of space exploration.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first argument in favour of or against space exploration.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second argument and decide which side is more convincing.
structure.conclusion: State your final position on whether space exploration is worth the investment.

content.modelAnswer:
Space exploration has captured human imagination for decades, but whether governments should continue to fund it is a matter of genuine debate. Two compelling arguments concern the scientific advances it produces and its enormous financial cost.

Space research has generated technological innovations that now benefit everyday life. Satellite communications, weather forecasting, improved medical imaging and water purification technologies all have roots in space exploration. These advances demonstrate that investment in space science can produce returns that far exceed the original purpose of the research.

Despite this, critics argue convincingly that the vast sums spent on space programmes could address more immediate human suffering. Poverty, disease and climate change continue to claim millions of lives each year, and governments arguably have a moral obligation to prioritise these issues over interplanetary ambitions.

In conclusion, while the technological benefits of space exploration are real, I believe addressing urgent problems on Earth must take precedence. Governments should seek ways to fund both priorities, but basic human welfare must always come first.
